channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Young women lean Democratic. Now the election may hinge on Harris' ability to run up the score
item.description: This year's presidential election could see a historic gender gap as young women line up behind Democrat Kamala Harris' bid to be the first female president.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein And Chris Megerian, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A look behind the scenes at the National Toy Hall of Fame
item.description: Before the National Toy Hall of Fame inducts a new class each fall, curators at the upstate New York attraction have to figure out how to showcase the honorees.
item.dc:creator: Carolyn Thompson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A man accused of threatening to kill Democratic election officials will likely plead guilty
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A man accused of repeatedly threatening to kill the top elections officials in Colorado and Arizona as well as judges and federal law enforcement agents is expected to plead guilty in federal court on Wednesday. Teak Ty Brockbank, 45, of Cortez, Colorado, has been jailed since his Aug.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: LeBron and Bronny James make history as the NBA's first father-son duo to play together
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — LeBron James and Bronny James became the first father and son to play in the NBA together Tuesday night during the Los Angeles Lakers ' season opener.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Surfer dies after being struck, impaled by swordfish
item.description: A longtime surfer and travel guide died after being impaled by a swordfish off the coast of Indonesia.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KREX 5

item.title: Court upholds freedom for woman whose conviction was overturned after 43 years behind bars
item.description: An appellate court in Missouri ruled Tuesday that a lower court was right when it decided to overturn the murder conviction of a woman who spent 43 years behind bars for a killing that her attorneys argue was committed by a discredited police officer.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Navy identifies 2 crew members killed in Washington state jet crash
item.description: The U.S. Navy identified the two pilots who died in a jet crash near Mount Rainier in Washington as Lt. Cmdr. Lyndsay P. Evans and Lt. Serena N. Wileman, both from California.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge tosses RNC challenge to Michigan voter roll maintenance
item.description: U.S. District Judge Jane Beckering ruled that neither the RNC nor two individual voters who joined the suit had legal standing to bring their case.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Rudy Giuliani ordered to turn over NYC apartment, 26 watches to Georgia election workers
item.description: A federal judge has ordered Rudy Giuliani to turn over his New York City apartment and various other assets to two Georgia election workers who won a $148 million defamation judgment against him.
item.dc:creator: Dave Collins, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Richard N. Winfield, First Amendment lawyer and former AP counsel, dies at 91
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Richard N. Winfield, a leading U.S. First Amendment lawyer who represented The Associated Press for three decades and championed freedom of expression for journalists around the world, died on Tuesday. He was 91.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge orders Giuliani to hand over assets to Georgia election workers
item.description: Rudy Giuliani must turn over much of his property — his apartment in New York, the city that made him mayor, and other prized possessions — to two Georgia election workers who have sought for months to collect their massive defamation judgment, a federal judge ruled Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 19-year-old woman found dead in oven in Canada Walmart
item.description: A Canadian Walmart store was closed for a third day Tuesday as police investigated the death of a woman whose body was found inside an oven.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, WGN 9

item.title: Insurance company refuses to pay 82-year-old Las Vegas woman's claim after her home burned down
item.description: Two weeks before the fire, the insurance company canceled the woman's policy, accusing her of running a motorcycle repair shop out of her home — something the 82-year-old woman denies.
item.dc:creator: Kyle J. Paine, KRON4

item.title: Judges reject GOP overseas ballot challenges in Michigan, North Carolina
item.description: State judges in Michigan and North Carolina on Monday rejected the Republican National Committee’s (RNC) separate challenges to certain overseas ballots in the key swing states.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: In battleground Georgia, some poor people see no reason to vote. That decision could sway election
item.description: MACON, Ga. (AP) — Sabrina Friday scanned the room at Mother's Nest, an organization in Macon that provides baby supplies, training, food and housing to mothers in need, and she asked how many planned to vote. Of the 30, mostly women, six raised their hands.
item.dc:creator: GARY FIELDS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump makes more debunked claims about FEMA as he visits storm damaged North Carolina
item.description: Trump was asked whether it was helpful to criticize hurricane relief workers after the Federal Emergency Management Agency recently paused work in the area because of reports they could be targeted by militia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Tracking the Tropics: Hurricane Oscar misses Florida in active October
item.description: Tropical Storm Oscar moves through the central and southeastern Bahamas, The National Hurricane Center said.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Harris speaks about creating economic opportunity for Latino men; Trump airs familiar grievances
item.description: Democratic presidential nominee Kamala Harris says she would work to bring more funds to community banks to help Latino men secure small business loans, while Republican Donald Trump’s economic roundtable aimed at Latino voters devolved Tuesday into a tirade of insults against his opponent.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Asian American boba brand finds opportunity after Simu Liu sparks cultural appropriation debate
item.description: Olivia Chen and Pauline Ang, friends and business partners on boba milk tea brand Twrl, have tried three times to get on “Shark Tank,” the ABC reality show where up-and-coming entrepreneurs try to woo big-name backers.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Eyewitness video captures frantic efforts to save lives after deadly collapse of dock walkway
item.description: SAVANNAH, Ga. (AP) — The metal gangway where dozens of people waited to board a ferry boat made a loud, creaking noise before snapping in the middle amid panicked cries from those sent plunging into the water.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM and EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge hears arguments to block Louisiana's Ten Commandments displays in schools
item.description: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — A federal judge heard arguments at a hearing Monday on whether he should temporarily block a new Louisiana law that requires the Ten Commandments to be displayed in every public school classroom by Jan. 1.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Delta crew member reportedly made Marine remove 'threatening' shirt
item.description: A Marine Corps veteran from the San Francisco Bay Area was temporarily removed from a Delta flight on Wednesday by a crew member who deemed her T-shirt to be “threatening,” according to a local report.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Helicopter crashes into radio tower in Houston, killing 4
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Four people aboard a helicopter were killed when it crashed into a radio tower in Houston and burst into flames, officials said. The helicopter went down just before 8 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Family demands answers after organs nearly taken from living man
item.description: A Kentucky family is looking for answers after a man was declared brain dead and being prepared to have his organs harvested while he was still alive.
item.dc:creator: Kiley Hill, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Still using 'LOL'? That could mean you are getting old
item.description: As time has seen several follow-up iterations, including ROFL and LMAO, the original "laugh out loud" acronym is a telltale sign that who you are talking to may be a millennial.
item.dc:creator: Juan Cisneros, ABC 4

item.title: Russell Wilson throws for 2 TDs, runs for another TD in Pittsburgh debut as Steelers rout Jets 37-15
item.description: PITTSBURGH (AP) — Russell Wilson sat at the podium, uniform still on, in no rush to move on from a moment he believed would always come. Even as the weeks passed and his aching calf struggled to get better. Even as Justin Fields deftly guided the Pittsburgh Steelers in his place.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Oscar brings winds, heavy rains to eastern Cuba after striking the Bahamas
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Tropical Storm Oscar brought heavy rains and winds to Cuba, an island already beleaguered by a massive power outage, late Sunday after brushing the Bahamas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Cooper scores in his Bills debut and Allen shakes off a sluggish start in 34-10 rout of Titans
item.description: It didn’t take Amari Cooper long to get the hang of Josh Allen and the Buffalo Bills' offense — even when the quarterback was changing plays at the line of scrimmage.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Singer among victims in couple's alleged Las Vegas theft spree during Super Bowl week
item.description: A married couple allegedly stole hundreds of thousands of dollars worth of valuables as Las Vegas hosted the Super Bowl, with one theft involving singer Jason Derulo.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, Fox 5

item.title: Grandma who texted stranger for Thanksgiving announces breast cancer diagnosis
item.description: The Arizona grandmother who sent a text message to what she believed was her grandson’s phone number, inviting him over for Thanksgiving dinner nearly nine years ago, has announced her diagnosis of breast cancer.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, ABC 4

item.title: One of the last Navajo Code Talkers from World War II dies at 107
item.description: John Kinsel Sr., one of the last remaining Navajo Code Talkers who transmitted messages during World War II based on the tribe's native language, has died. He was 107.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New Zealand wins cricket's Women’s T20 World Cup for 1st time with 32-run victory over South Africa
item.description: New Zealand won the Women’s T20 World Cup for the first time, beating South Africa by 32 runs on Sunday after a standout performance from Amelia Kerr with bat and ball.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: In New York, a constitutional amendment provides election fodder for the left and the right
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — Democrats pushed to get a constitutional amendment on New York's ballot because they believed it could energize liberals eager to protect abortion rights.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Many gift-buying parents pressured to overspend this holiday season: Survey
item.description: (NerdWallet) - Holiday shopping season is upon us, and the pressure is on, or at least it feels that way for some parents.
item.dc:creator: Erin El Issa, ABC 4

item.title: A drone targets the Israeli prime minister's house during new barrages with Hezbollah
item.description: Israel's government said a drone targeted Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s house Saturday, with no casualties, as fighting with Lebanon-based Hezbollah and Gaza -based Hamas showed no pause after the killing of the Hamas mastermind of last year’s Oct. 7 attack.
item.dc:creator: ADAM SCHRECK and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: American Airlines flight taken out of service after suffering engine damage while taxiing
item.description: An American Airlines flight arriving in Chicago from overseas was taken out of service after it suffered damage to its engine while taxiing to the gate at O'Hare on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Friends and musicians express heartbreak at the death of singer Liam Payne at age 31
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Friends, collaborators and fellow musicians expressed shock and sadness Thursday over the death of former One Direction singer Liam Payne, who died in Buenos Aires, Argentina Wednesday at age 31. A look at some of the reactions: __ "We are heartbroken.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
